exemplar.title: Investigation Into the Impact of Visual Information on Memory Recall

detailedExemplarData.title: Investigation Into the Impact of Visual Information on Memory Recall

subject.title: Psychology
subject.description:
IB Psychology is a Group 3 subject in the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP). The course examines human behavior and mental processes through scientific inquiry, exploring biological, cognitive, and sociocultural approaches to understanding behavior. Students develop critical thinking skills while investigating contemporary psychological research, research methods, and ethical considerations in psychological practice.

The course is available at Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours) and Higher Level (HL, 240 hours), with first assessment in 2027.

Assessment consists of Paper 1 (integration of concepts, content, and contexts through short-answer and extended-response questions: SL 2 hours, HL 2 hours, SL 50%, HL 40%), Paper 2 (application of psychological knowledge to research contexts: SL 1 hour, HL 2 hours, SL 25%, HL 20%), Paper 3 (HL only: research methods and data analysis, 1 hour, 20%), and Internal Assessment (research proposal development: SL 25%, HL 20%).

strengths: Clear, precise aim with direct relevance to educational practice
Accurate description and linkage of Dual Coding and Sensory Semantic theories
Fully operationalized IV and DV in both null and alternative hypotheses
weaknesses: Theoretical connections could be elaborated to explain deeper referential links
Relevance to broader memory research beyond classroom context is lightly touched
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The introduction is strong overall, with a wellâstated, relevant aim and accurate theoretical grounding. The hypotheses are fully operationalized, demonstrating academic rigour. Minor depth is lacking in theoretical detail and broader significance, which prevents a perfect 6, but performance solidly sits in the top band at a 5.

strengths: Independent-groups design is not only described but justified to control order effects
Opportunity sampling and participant characteristics are clearly outlined
Materials and controlled variables are described with some rationale
weaknesses: Sampling justification is brief and lacks critical evaluation of generalisability
Explanation of how controls uphold internal validity could be expanded
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration section moves beyond mere description, offering justification for the design and materials. However, the sampling rationale and internal validity controls are only partially explained. Overall, this performance sits solidly in the explanatory band but does not fully exploit the top mark, meriting a 3.

strengths: Appropriate selection and correct application of descriptive and inferential statistics
Graphical presentation addresses the hypothesis with error bars and condition comparison
Findings are linked to hypothesis and statistical outcome
weaknesses: Minor typographical errors in the statistical write-up (e.g., p-value notation)
Graph image is pixelated and lacks clear axis labels
No effectâsize measure reported to contextualize practical significance
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The analysis demonstrates strong methodological choice and interpretation, with correct use of Mann-Whitney U and clear linkage to the hypothesis. Presentation issues and the absence of effectâsize metrics, however, limit the work from reaching full accuracy. This places the analysis in the upper middle of the highest band, justifying a 4.

strengths: Findings are discussed in reference to Dual Coding and Sensory Semantic theories
Strengths and limitations are relevant and explained in terms of validity
Modifications are explicitly linked to limitations and justified for improved design
weaknesses: Discussion of theoretical mechanisms lacks depth (e.g., referential links)
Ethical considerations and participant welfare are only lightly addressed
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The evaluation section robustly connects findings to theory and offers wellâjustified modifications. Explanation of strengths and limitations is clear, but the theoretical discussion could be deeper and ethical aspects need more attention. The work sits near the top band but does not fully satisfy its highest level, earning a 4.

strandSelections.reasoning: The bar chart displays means with error bars and directly compares the two conditions, clearly addressing the hypothesis. Axes are labelled in the figure caption, yet the graphic embedded is slightly pixelated and axis titles in the picture are not visible in the report extract. These minor presentation issues justify a 5 rather than 6.

The student states the statistical outcome, links it to rejection of the null hypothesis, and comments on the pattern of means, fully connecting interpretation to both data and hypothesis. Discussion of effect size is absent, so a strong 5 (not 6) is appropriate.

Both null and alternative hypotheses are clearly written. The IV (type of material: VD vs VDVR) and DV (number of facts recalled out of 42) are not only named but operationalised with measurable levels and scoring rules. No ambiguity is present, meriting the top of the highest band.

Opportunity sampling and participant characteristics are named and some rationale is offered (availability, English proficiency, educational context). However, justification remains brief and does not fully explore how these choices influence validity or generalisability, so a low-mid score in the explanatory band (3) is most fitting.

Dual Coding Theory and Sensory Semantic Theory are accurately described and explicitly linked to the predicted benefit of combined visual-verbal input. The write-up demonstrates a sound conceptual grasp, yet some theoretical detail (e.g., separate codes, referential connections) is abbreviated and citations minimal, so a solid 5 rather than 6 is justified.

Findings are clearly discussed in relation to Dual Coding Theory, Sensory Semantic Theory, and the replicated study. The theoretical implications are stated but not explored in depth (e.g., no detailed mechanism analysis), warranting a 5 within the top band.

The student states a precise aim â to examine whether adding visual information to verbal descriptions improves recall in pre-IB students â and explicitly explains why this is relevant for educational practice (e.g., informs classroom teaching techniques). The explanation is clear but concise; while educational relevance is mentioned, broader psychological significance (e.g., contribution to memory research) is only lightly touched, so the middle of the top band (5) is appropriate rather than full marks.

Several relevant strengths and limitations (design, sample, stimuli) are explained rather than merely listed, demonstrating how each point affects validity or generalisability. Coverage is good but not exhaustive (e.g., ethical issues lightly handled), so 5 is suitable.

Key controlled variable (length/simplicity of verbal descriptions) and the materials (AI-generated faces, two PowerPoints, distraction crossword) are described and partially justified (neutrality, accessibility). Some further explanation of how controls uphold internal validity is given but not extensive, warranting a 3 within the explanatory band.

Proposed modifications (switch to repeated measures, widen sample, use realistic images) are explicitly linked to identified limitations and justified in terms of improving reliability or ecological validity. While sound, the justifications remain brief, placing the work at 5 rather than 6.

The independent-groups design is not only identified but the choice is justified (avoids order effects, demand characteristics). Limitations of the design are acknowledged. This constitutes an explanation rather than mere description, fitting the top of the Exploration rubric.

Appropriate descriptive (mean, SD) and inferential statistics (Mann-Whitney U for small, independent, non-parametric data) are correctly selected and applied. Minor typographical slips ("p < 0.5") appear but do not affect the calculation or conclusion, so a 5 reflects strong but not flawless execution.

annotations.comment: Hypotheses are precisely formulated with operationalised IV and DV. The inclusion of both null and alternative hypotheses demonstrates academic completeness.
The sentence ending âraised withâ is incomplete, leaving the context ambiguous; ensure all introductory paragraphs are fully presented.
The title succinctly conveys the independent and dependent variables and frames the investigation within memory recall, establishing clear focus and relevance.
The description of Dual Coding Theory and Sensory Semantic Theory is accurate and well linked to memory processes, showing solid theoretical grounding.
Theoretical connections lack depth in explaining referential links between visual and verbal codes; elaborating on how the dual subsystems interact would strengthen the rationale.
Consider adding citations for key theoretical points and distinguishing sources for DCT and SST to enhance academic rigor.
The aim and relevance are clearly stated, but you might expand on its broader contribution to memory research beyond educational practice.
Descriptive statistics (mean, SD) are appropriate for ratio data with a small sample, showing correct initial analysis choices.
Presentation of Table 1 lacks visible decimal alignment and uses commas for decimals; adopting period notation and APA formatting would improve clarity.
Graph in Figure 1 addresses the hypothesis but is slightly pixelated and missing clear axis labels; ensure all visuals are crisp and labeled.
Consider adding an effect-size measure (e.g., r for Mann-Whitney U) to deepen interpretation of the practical significance.
The hypothesis text could be more concise; consider rephrasing to follow standard null-hypothesis notation, e.g., âH0: Î¼VDVR = Î¼VD.â
Independent-groups design is justified to avoid order effects. This clear explanation demonstrates an understanding of design trade-offs.
Opportunity sampling rationale is brief; explain how this may limit generalisability or introduce bias to validity discussions.
Ethical considerations are noted, but the impact of power dynamics or assent procedures for minors could be discussed further.
Control of verbal description length is mentioned but not fully explained; clarify how this controls for participant proficiency and ensures internal validity.
Materials description identifies AI-generated faces and presentations; explaining how random selection upholds stimulus neutrality reinforces methodological rigor.
The procedure section is thorough, but the rationale for the fiveâminute delay could be linked to memory consolidation theory to strengthen rationale.
Bibliographic entries vary in completeness; include full retrieval dates, DOIs, and formatted author names consistently to meet academic standards.
Online resource entries should specify access dates uniformly (e.g., day/month/year) and include authorship when available to improve traceability.
The outlier decision is justified, but consider discussing whether winsorizing or robust statistics could mitigate its impact.
Discussion connects results to Dual Coding and Sensory Semantic theories, showing solid interpretation within theoretical frameworks.
Depth of theoretical discussion is moderate; elaborating on mechanisms (e.g., referential connections) could enrich the findings discussion.
Strengths and limitations are relevant and linked to validity; discussing ethical dimensions or participant comfort would complete the evaluation.
Modifications are logically justified by limitations; consider also justifying sample size increases with power analysis to optimise design.
